summary.business: Ambiq Micro designs and sells ultra-low power semiconductor systems-on-chip (SoCs) and associated software that enable AI and general compute at the edge, leveraging its proprietary SPOT platform to deliver two to five times lower power consumption than conventional designs. The company monetizes through direct sales and a distributor network, selling chips into personal devices (smartwatches, AR/VR, smart rings), medical/healthcare, industrial, and smart home markets, with over 300 million devices powered to date.
summary.sector: Semiconductors — Edge AI / Ultra-Low Power SoCs
summary.financials: Net sales of $25.1M for Q1 2026 vs. $15.7M in Q1 2025 (60% YoY growth). Full-year 2025 net sales of $72.5M vs. $76.1M in 2024 (4.7% decline). Sales to U.S., Europe, and Asia ex-China grew 46.4% in Q1 2026 and 74.2% in 2025. The prospectus excerpt does not state whether the company is profitable.
summary.useOfProceeds: The prospectus excerpt does not specify the intended use of proceeds; the Use of Proceeds section is referenced but not included in the provided text.
risks: Revenue concentration in body-worn AI devices and personal electronics
Decline in total revenue from 2024 to 2025 despite growth in ex-China markets
Evolving and uncertain global AI regulatory landscape
Dependence on proprietary SPOT technology facing competition from larger semiconductor companies

summary.business: Ambiq Micro designs and sells ultra-low power semiconductor systems-on-chip (SoCs) and integrated software that enable AI and general compute at the edge, using its proprietary SPOT platform to cut power consumption two to five times versus conventional designs. The company monetizes by selling these SoC solutions into personal devices, medical/healthcare, industrial, and smart home markets, powering over 290 million devices to date.
